LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUN-04-2020 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT FOR $8,944.62. ORDER SIGNED. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO HAVE A JUDGMENT ENTERED IN THIS CASE, PLAINTIFF MUST PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED JUDGMENT TO THE COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. TRISTAN P. ESPINOZA, Esq. of Reese Law Group (Tel: 760-842-5850) appearing via CourtCall for the plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff maintains its initial summary judgment burden per Code of Civil Procedure 437c. The court overrules defendant's objections to the Gelbowitz declaration. Gelbowitz lays a foundation regarded the matters attested to and properly authenticates the business records attached to her declaration, which show that defendant is in default on her credit card account. (See Evid. Code 1400, 1401 [authentication], 1271 [business records exception to hearsay rule]; Unifund CCR, LLC v. Dear (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1, 7-9 [trial court has broad discretion in admitting business records, bank records are in a different category than normal business records, and a qualified witness need not be the custodian of the business record for it to be admissible].) Plaintiff is entitled to judgment for $8,944.62.

Order signed. If plaintiff wishes to have a judgment entered in this case, plaintiff must prepare and submit a proposed judgment to the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Mar-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED

Appearances: Tristan Espinosa of the Reese Law Group (760.842.5850) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

Mr. Espinosa stipulates to a decision by the Judge Pro Tem. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's unopposed Motion to Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted ("Motion") is granted. Under the Discovery Act, court are "required to grant a deemed admission motion" where the responding party fails to serve a verified response "before the hearing on that motion." (Tobin v. Oris (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 814, 828, disapproved on other grounds of by Wilcox v. Birtwhistle (1999) 21 Cal.4th 973.) Defendant failed to serve a verified response and did not oppose the Motion. Accordingly, the Motion is granted. Mr. Espinosa is referred to department 206 regarding his request to continue the trial date. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/JPT)
